hero.title: Villa Ester
hero.subtitle: From the Zerauscheks of Zara to Maria Callas — a house on Lake Garda

media.alt: Sirmione peninsula jutting into Lake Garda, seen from above
media.caption: Sirmione — the narrow peninsula on Lake Garda where Giannantoni built his villa

media.alt: The medieval Scaliger castle at the entrance to Sirmione
media.caption: Antonio Zerauschek acquired the villa at the height of his interwar career in Zara

media.src: media/docs/Fulvia Zerauschek at Villa Ester Sirmione.png
media.alt: Fulvia Zerauschek sitting on the loggia balustrade of Villa Ester, Sirmione, viewed through open French doors
media.caption: Fulvia Zerauschek at Villa Ester — the loggia, the marble floors, the conifer park beyond

media.src: media/docs/Difesa Adriatica 1973 Antonio Zerauschek obituary Zara Florence.jpeg
media.alt: Difesa Adriatica obituary for Antonio Zerauschek, 1973 — exile newspaper of the Zara community
media.caption: War, bombing, exile — the Zerauscheks left Zara and sold the Sirmione villa

media.alt: Villa Ester exterior from the road, Sirmione, c. 1958 — three floors, solarium turret, two 1950s cars
media.caption: Meneghini bought the villa for sixty million lire — a gift for his wife, Maria Callas

media.alt: The Lombard-era church of San Pietro in Mavino, Sirmione, beside the villa grounds
media.caption: 'Telefoni, telefoni. Rose, rose.' — Callas directed the restoration herself

media.src: media/docs/Villa Callas Sirmione magazine clipping catapecchia c1959.png
media.alt: Italian magazine clipping showing Villa Callas exterior and inset portrait of Maria Callas, c. 1959
media.caption: 'Questa villa è una catapecchia' — Callas called it a hovel; the press disagreed

media.alt: Villa Callas exterior, Via Caio Valerio Catullo 7, Sirmione — unchanged since the 1950s
media.caption: Villa Callas today — the facade unchanged, a plaque for Callas and Meneghini on the wall
